title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day. You want to maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy one stock and choosing a different day in the future to sell that stock. Return the maximum profit you can achieve from this transaction. If you cannot achieve any profit, return 0.
hints: Keep track of the minimum price seen so far.
For each day, calculate the profit if you sold on that day.
Update the maximum profit if the current profit is greater.

title: Maximum Average Subarray I
description: You are given an integer array nums consisting of n elements, and an integer k. Find a contiguous subarray whose length is equal to k that has the maximum average value. Return the maximum average value. The answer will be computed with a floating-point number precision of 10^-5.
hints: Compute the sum of the first window of size k.
Slide the window by adding the next element and removing the first element.
Track the maximum window sum and divide by k at the end.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
hints: Use a sliding window with two pointers.
Use a hash set to track characters in the current window.
Shrink the window from the left when a duplicate is found.
spaceComplexity: O(min(n, m)) where m is the character set size

title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
description: You are given a string s consisting of uppercase English letters and an integer k. You can choose at most k characters from the string and replace them with any uppercase English letter. Return the length of the longest substring containing the same letter you can get after performing the replacement.
hints: The number of characters to replace is window length minus the count of the most frequent character.
Keep a frequency map of characters in the current window.
Shrink the window when the number of replacements needed exceeds k.
spaceComplexity: O(1) since there are 26 uppercase letters

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given two strings s and t of lengths m and n respectively, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t (including duplicates) is included in the window. If there is no such substring, return the empty string. The testcases will be generated such that the answer is unique.
hints: Count the frequency of each character in t.
Use two pointers to maintain a valid window.
A window is valid when it contains all characters of t with required frequencies.
spaceComplexity: O(1) since there are 128 ASCII characters

title: Permutation in String
description: Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise. In other words, return true if one of s1's permutations is the substring of s2.
hints: A permutation of s1 has the same character frequency as s1.
Use a fixed-size sliding window of length equal to s1.
Compare character frequency arrays of the window and s1.
timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the length of s2
spaceComplexity: O(1) since there are 26 lowercase letters

title: Find All Anagrams in a String
description: Given two strings s and p, return an array of all the start indices of p's anagrams in s. You may return the answer in any order. An Anagram is a word or phrase formed by rearranging the letters of a different word or phrase, typically using all the original letters exactly once.
hints: Use a fixed-size sliding window of length equal to p.
Compare character frequency arrays of the window and p.
If they match, record the starting index.
timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the length of s
spaceComplexity: O(1) since there are 26 lowercase letters

title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
description: Given an array of positive integers nums and a positive integer target, return the minimal length of a subarray whose sum is greater than or equal to target. If there is no such subarray, return 0 instead.
hints: Use a sliding window that expands to the right.
When the window sum >= target, try to shrink from the left.
Track the minimum window length that satisfies the condition.

title: Fruits Into Baskets
description: You are visiting a farm that has a single row of fruit trees arranged from left to right. The trees are represented by an integer array fruits where fruits[i] is the type of fruit the ith tree produces. You want to collect as much fruit as possible. You have two baskets, and each basket can only hold one type of fruit. Starting from any tree, collect fruit from each tree until you cannot. Return the maximum number of fruits you can collect.
hints: This is equivalent to finding the longest subarray with at most 2 distinct elements.
Use a hash map to count the frequency of fruits in the current window.
Shrink the window when there are more than 2 distinct fruit types.
spaceComplexity: O(1) since there are at most 3 distinct fruit types in the map at any time

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: You are given an array of integers nums, there is a sliding window of size k which is moving from the very left of the array to the very right. You can only see the k numbers in the window. Each time the sliding window moves right by one position. Return the max sliding window.
hints: Use a deque to maintain indices of elements in decreasing order.
Remove indices from the back of the deque if their values are smaller than the current element.
Remove indices from the front of the deque if they are outside the window.

title: Substring with Concatenation of All Words
description: You are given a string s and an array of strings words of the same length. Return all starting indices of substrings in s that are a concatenation of each word in words exactly once and without any intervening characters. You can return the answer in any order.
hints: The total length of each concatenated substring is words.length * words[0].length.
Use a frequency map for words.
For each starting position, check if you can form a concatenation of all words.
timeComplexity: O(n * m) where n is the length of s and m is the number of words
spaceComplexity: O(m * k) where k is the length of each word
